reasoning: The document suffers from severe content loss, specifically the omission of pages 21-32 (Goals 2-5), which is the core of the report. This is corroborated by a very low table_count_ratio (0.25) and an LLM visual score of 0.47, indicating that while text recall appears high for the processed sections, the structural and graphical integrity of the document is compromised.

concerns.analysis: The output is missing a significant block of core content. While an omission marker ('_omitted') correctly notes the removal of sections between pages 4 and 14 for token budget reasons, a subsequent large block of content is missing without any marker. Specifically, pages 21 through 32, which contain the detailed progress reports for Goal 2 ('Transform the delivery of services'), Goal 3 ('Optimize and secure...'), Goal 4 ('Promote an inclusive...'), Goal 5 ('Leverage data assets...'), 'PRIVACY',
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PDF pages 21-32 containing Goal 2 ('Transform the delivery of services'), Goal 3 ('Optimize and secure the state’s IT and application portfolios'), Goal 4 ('Promote an inclusive and innovative workforce'), Goal 5 ('Leverage data assets and analytics to further advance a data-driven government'), 'PRIVACY', and 'HEALTH CARE'.
concerns.output_quote: The output transitions directly from the end of Goal 1 ('...built NC.gov as a constituent portal that transforms how users can access all state services.') to the 'Awards and Recognitions' section heading.
concerns.location: Sections between element index 14 and 15, corresponding to PDF pages 21-32.

concerns.description: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
concerns.analysis: There is a significant mismatch between the number of headings in the semantic.json (75) and those present in the rendered HTML (96). This difference of 21 headings (approx. 28%) exceeds the allowable 10% threshold, indicating that the renderer has injected additional heading elements not defined in the structured data.

The visual organization of the budget data on pages 14 and 15 has been significantly altered. In the source PDF, these pages feature pie charts to visually represent proportions. In the HTML output, these charts are entirely absent and have been replaced with text-based lists/tables. This collapse of a graphical data visualization into a text list significantly changes the visual flow and the way a user interprets the comparative data.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 14 and 15 contain pie charts for 'BUDGET EXPENDITURES' and 'BUDGET FUNDING SOURCES'.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output renders these sections as plain text lists with percentages, losing the visual organization of the data visualization.

concerns.analysis: Multiple meaningful images from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. This includes the two pie charts on pages 14 and 15, the complex 'N.C. Health Information Exchange Authority' governance structure diagram on page 32, and the FBI certificate image on page 40. These are functional images that convey information or recognition and their absence is a major defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: In the source PDF: Page 14 (Expenditures pie chart), Page 15 (Funding Sources pie chart), Page 32 (Governance structure flowchart/diagram), Page 40 (FBI certificate image).
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output contains text placeholders or labels for these items (e.g., 'The governance structure for the N.C. Health Information Exchange Authority') but the actual images/diagrams are missing.

concerns.analysis: The overall visual quality is degraded by the omission of the icons in the 'OUR GUIDING PRINCIPLES' sidebar on page 5. While the text is present, the visual identity of this section is lost without the corresponding icons (people, gear, etc.) shown in the source. This is a minor quality issue compared to the major missing images and layout changes.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 5 shows a vertical sidebar 'OUR GUIDING PRINCIPLES' with seven distinct circular icons next to principle names.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output shows the principles list but all icons are missing.
concerns.location: Vision, Mission and Priorities section, page 5

action_items: Investigate the cause of the 11-page content gap to see if it was a token-limit truncation.
Restore missing pie charts and governance diagrams as accessible SVG or high-quality alt-text containers.
Reconcile the heading structure to match the source document hierarchy.
